title: Do not default to testimonial carousels
summary: AI-generated testimonial sections consistently use a horizontal carousel of quote cards with circular headshots, star ratings, dot indicators, and auto-rotation — a pattern users have learned to ignore.
instruction: Avoid auto-rotating testimonial carousels. Present social proof in a format that users can control: static grid, featured case study, full quote list, or video testimonial. If a carousel is used, disable auto-rotation.
rationale: Testimonial carousels suffer from banner-blindness — users have learned to ignore auto-rotating content. The identical card format suggests stock imagery rather than genuine endorsements. Star ratings appear even for contexts where they are not the appropriate trust signal.
violations: Horizontal carousel with left/right arrow buttons and dot indicators
Circular headshots with identical sizing across all testimonials
Large decorative quotation marks in accent color
Star ratings on every card even for B2B enterprise testimonials
controls: Static grid of testimonial quotes with no rotation
Featured case study format with video or metrics
Simple quote list without avatars or decorative elements
One prominent testimonial instead of a collection
borderlineCases: Consumer product pages with verified user reviews needing aggregate display
contextualExceptions: Sites where carousel interaction is the expected design language
detectorSignal: A set of horizontally arranged quote cards with navigation arrows and dot indicators, containing quotation marks and headshot images.
matcherProposal: Detect a horizontal scrolling container with multiple child cards containing quote marks (or blockquote elements), navigation arrows, and dot indicators.
detectorLimitations: Cannot determine if testimonials are authentic or generated
Cannot assess whether carousel is necessary for space constraints
falsePositiveScenarios: High-traffic consumer sites with sufficient testimonial volume to justify carousel
mitigationStrategy: Flag with auto-rotation as a stronger signal than static carousel. Suggest static grid as alternative.

fixturePlans.violation: Auto-rotating testimonial carousel with headshots, star ratings, decorative quotes, and dot indicators.
fixturePlans.control: Static 2-column grid of quote cards with name, title, and company — no rotation, no stars, no decorative quotes.
fixturePlans.borderline: Consumer review widget with star ratings where users expect the carousel format.

cleanRoomAttestations: Developed without access to proprietary prompt collections.
overlapNotes: Partly covered by motion.transition-all when auto-rotation uses transition animations.
